element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Cap-and-trade system allows player customization of four roles: scout, wizard, fighter, priest
Players can wander entire game world but encounters are unwise early; game is intentionally brutal in early phase
Regular chance encounters; can occur while stationary, fundamentally different from era-typical turn-based RPGs
Automated by default but allows micromanagement of four combatants; player must manage defense magic, positioning, weapon switching
Spawns fatal Shadow Man-like enemy; no experience reward, signaling mechanic is penalized
Item acquisition system; weapons, armor, healing items, rings, miscellaneous (torches)
Self-contained levels with obstacles, loot, enemies, maze-like architecture; must be revisited, function like Zelda temples
Players can be attacked while viewing constellations; adds atmospheric unpredictability
Dead party members create crippling disadvantage; players incentivized to journey to Anak holy temples for revival

typical_relief_triggers: Successful monster defeat and experience gain
Reaching tavern or merchant for item acquisition
Fleeing battle via L/R bumpers (SNES version)
typical_tension_triggers: Combat against 100+ enemy variants with unique attack patterns
Encounters with unexpectedly fast zigzagging enemies
Shadow Man emergence from ground with ominous music
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Early-game brutality with surreal enemy encounters; real-time tactics demand constant micromanagement; unpredictable ambush mechanics (stationary attacks, merchant encounters, urn triggers) create constant low-level tension punctuated by combat spikes

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Urns scattered throughout world; kicking them is optional but heavily penalized, signaling optional exploration trap mechanic
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Half-buried urns contain deadly encounters; players learn through punishment rather than prior knowledge

difficulty_curve.early_game: Extremely brutal; game designed to punish unprepared players; early encounters with urn-spawned enemies are quickly fatal to unaware players; lacks supplemental context (Gary Gygax story book not included in SNES version)
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Moderated by player leveling and castle progression; acquiring equipment and understanding enemy patterns reduces brutality; mandatory castle visits provide structure
notable_spikes: Party member death creating crippling disadvantage

notable_level_patterns.description: Zelda-like structured levels with maze-like architecture, obstacles, loot, and enemies; must be visited at least once and can be revisited; act as progression gates
Traveling merchants ambush party in open world; urns trigger deadly encounters; night sky viewing creates combat risk; establishes constant threat even outside formal dungeons
Scattered urn-kicking traps that spawn unique fatal enemies with no experience reward; designed to discourage casual exploration
